title: The Solutions Architect Professional Role
description:
## What an AWS Solutions Architect Professional Does

The **Solutions Architect Professional (SAP)** designs and evolves complex, often multi-account workloads on AWS. Where the Associate level focuses on building a single well-architected workload, the **Professional** level is about *organizational complexity*: many accounts, hybrid connectivity, migration of large estates, cost governance across teams, and continuous improvement of existing architectures. You are expected to weigh trade-offs (cost vs. resilience vs. operational effort) and justify decisions, not just recall feature lists.

### AWS Global Infrastructure

| Layer | What it is | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **Region** | A separate geographic area (e.g. `eu-west-1` Ireland) with its own isolated set of data centers. | Data residency, latency to users, service availability. Most services are region-scoped. |
| **Availability Zone (AZ)** | One or more discrete data centers within a Region, with independent power/cooling/networking, interconnected by low-latency links. | Spreading across AZs gives **high availability** inside a single Region. |
| **Edge location / PoP** | Hundreds of points of presence used by **CloudFront**, Route 53, and AWS Global Accelerator. | Caches content and terminates connections **close to users** for low latency. |
| **Local Zones / Wavelength** | Infrastructure placed in metro areas / telco networks. | Single-digit-millisecond latency for specialized workloads. |

> A Region typically has **3 or more AZs**. "Multi-AZ" = resilient within one Region. "Multi-Region" = resilient against a whole-Region failure, at higher complexity and cost.

### The account as a boundary

An **AWS account** is the fundamental unit of **billing isolation** and **security isolation**. Resources in one account are invisible to another unless you explicitly share or grant cross-account access. Professional architectures almost always use **many** accounts (organized with AWS Organizations) rather than one large account, to separate environments (dev/prod), limit blast radius, and delegate ownership.

### How this course maps to SAP-C02

The official exam has four domains:

1. **Design Solutions for Organizational Complexity** (multi-account, networking, governance)
2. **Design for New Solutions**
3. **Continuous Improvement for Existing Solutions**
4. **Accelerate Workload Migration and Modernization**

This subject covers the foundations, networking, and organizational-complexity material. This learning platform also offers a broader **DevOps** subject (CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, observability) that complements the architecture content here.

When you're comfortable with this vocabulary, mark the lesson complete and continue.
